name: debug-oidc-trust
description: Debug GitHub OIDC federation trust policy failures when GitHub Actions cannot assume the AWS deploy role.

preconditions.description: AWS CLI is installed and configured with admin access
GitHub Actions workflow has id-token: write permission

steps.command: aws iam list-open-id-connect-providers --output json | grep token.actions.githubusercontent.com
steps.description: Verify OIDC provider exists — if no output, the GitHub OIDC provider has not been created. Run the infra/github-oidc.ts component via SST to create it.

steps.command: aws iam get-open-id-connect-provider --open-id-connect-provider-arn $(aws iam list-open-id-connect-providers --query 'OpenIDConnectProviderList[?contains(Arn, `token.actions.githubusercontent.com`)].Arn' --output text) --output json
steps.description: Inspect OIDC provider configuration — verify clientIdList includes 'sts.amazonaws.com' and thumbprintList is set.

steps.command: aws iam get-role --role-name GitHubDeployRole --query 'Role.AssumeRolePolicyDocument' --output json 2>/dev/null || echo 'Role not found'
steps.description: Check trust policy conditions — verify StringLike conditions match the repo, branch, and environment claims. The sub claim format is 'repo:OWNER/REPO:ref:refs/heads/BRANCH' or 'repo:OWNER/REPO:environment:ENV'.

steps.command: echo 'In a failing GitHub Actions run, add this step to inspect the OIDC token claims:' && echo '  - run: |' && echo '      TOKEN=$(curl -sS -H "Authorization: bearer $ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN" "$ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL&audience=sts.amazonaws.com" | jq -r .value)' && echo '      echo "$TOKEN" | cut -d. -f2 | base64 -d 2>/dev/null | jq .' && echo 'Key claims to verify: sub (must match trust policy), aud (must be sts.amazonaws.com), repository, ref, environment'
steps.description: Validate GitHub token claims — decode the OIDC token in a workflow to verify the sub, aud, repository, ref, and environment claims match the trust policy conditions.

steps.command: aws iam simulate-principal-policy --policy-source-arn $(aws iam get-role --role-name GitHubDeployRole --query 'Role.Arn' --output text) --action-names s3:PutObject cloudformation:CreateStack ssm:GetParameter secretsmanager:GetSecretValue --output json
steps.description: Check IAM role permissions — simulate key actions the deploy role needs. Verify SST deploy permissions (CloudFormation, S3, Lambda) and secrets access (SSM, Secrets Manager).

validation.description: Deploy role can be assumed from GitHub Actions
validation.command: Check the GitHub Actions workflow run log for 'Assuming role with OIDC' success message
validation.expect: No 'AccessDenied' or 'Not authorized to perform sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity' errors

validation.description: SST deploy succeeds after trust fix
validation.command: Trigger a deploy workflow run and verify it completes without OIDC errors
validation.expect: Workflow run completes with green status

rollback: OIDC trust policy changes are non-destructive. If the trust policy is too broad, tighten the StringLike conditions. If too narrow, add the missing repo/branch/environment subjects.
